para.bold: define fermion as sign -1 and boson as sign +1
Define a vector of GF_pair objects
Define a vector of references of Green's functions
Define a vector of Green's functions

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< double > &data_vec, std::string fname, int prec)
memberdef.name: appendData

memberdef.name: Bubble1_ness

memberdef.name: Bubble1_ness

memberdef.name: Bubble2_ness

memberdef.name: Bubble2_ness

memberdef.argsstring: (GF &G, dos_function &dos, double beta, double h, int limit, int nn, double mu)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_les_ness

memberdef.argsstring: (GF_pair &G, dos_function &dos, double beta, double h, int limit, int nn, double mu)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_les_ness

memberdef.argsstring: (GF &G, dos_function &dos, double beta, double h, int limit, int nn, double mu)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ness

para.bold: Equilibrium steady state propagator for the given density of states

blockquote.para: Calculate the steady state equilibrium GF $ G $ for the given density of states (own type dos_function) via $G(t-t') = -i \int d\omega A(\omega+\mu) \exp(i\omega (t'-t)) [ \Theta(t-t') - \Theta(t'-t)\exp(-\beta*\omega)]$, for all times and the retarded and lesser component. The calculation uses an adaptive Fourier transform algorithm.

parameterdescription.para: The output Greens function GF set to the equilibrium free propagator
max number of intervals in Fourier transform (default: 100)
number of points in each interval of the Fourier transform (default: 20)

memberdef.argsstring: (GF_pair &G, dos_function &dos, double beta, double h, int limit, int nn, double mu)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ness

para.bold: Equilibrium steady state propagator for the given density of states

blockquote.para: Calculate the steady state equilibrium GF_pair $ G $ (in time domain) for the given density of states (own type dos_function) via $G(t-t') = -i \int d\omega A(\omega+\mu) \exp(i\omega (t'-t)) [ \Theta(t-t') - \Theta(t'-t)\exp(-\beta*\omega)]$, for all times and the retarded and lesser component. The calculation uses an adaptive Fourier transform algorithm.

parameterdescription.para: The output Greens function pair GF_pair set to the equilibrium free propagator (time component)
max number of intervals in Fourier transform (default: 100)
number of points in each interval of the Fourier transform (default: 20)

memberdef.argsstring: (GF &G, dos_function &dos, double h, int limit, int nn, double mu)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ret_ness

memberdef.argsstring: (GF_pair &G, dos_function &dos, double h, int limit, int nn, double mu)
memberdef.name: green_equilibrium_ret_ness
